task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'fragment_settings.xml' in the Android Studio project explorer under the 'res/layout' directory, or open as a tab in the editor.
Look for 'SettingsFragment.kt' or 'SettingsFragment.java' open in the editor or visible in the project explorer under the 'com.example.tasktracker' package.

task_completion.description: Add settingsFragment destination to the navigation graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the navigation graph file (e.g., nav_graph.xml) open in the editor. Visually, there should be a 'settingsFragment' destination in the Design view, or XML code showing '<fragment android:id="@+id/settingsFragment"'.

task_completion.description: Add action_home_to_settings action to the navigation graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the navigation graph editor, look for an arrow connecting the home fragment to the settings fragment in the Design view, or XML code inside the home fragment showing '<action android:id="@+id/action_home_to_settings" app:destination="@id/settingsFragment"'.

task_completion.description: Implement click listener in HomeFragment to navigate to Settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'HomeFragment' open in the editor with code setting an onClickListener on 'btn_settings' (or its binding equivalent) that calls a navigation method like 'findNavController().navigate(R.id.action_home_to_settings)'.
Look for a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message in the Android Studio Build/Run tool window, a green checkmark on the build task, or the app successfully running in an emulator showing the UI.

integrity.description: Agent used Android Studio GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Android Studio interface being used to edit files, view the navigation graph, and run builds, rather than just terminal commands like 'echo' or 'sed'.

integrity.description: Agent wrote actual Android code and XML
integrity.visual_evidence: The files should contain valid Kotlin/Java and XML syntax with correct Android imports and tags, not just pasted text from the task description.

integrity.description: Agent actually ran the Gradle build
integrity.visual_evidence: The Build tool window in Android Studio should show genuine Gradle execution logs and tasks (e.g., 'Executing tasks: [:app:assembleDebug]'), not a mocked terminal output.
